name: trimodal-diffusion
description: Systematically analyze design choices for tri-modal (text, image, audio) masked diffusion models trained from scratch. Provides scaling laws, optimal modality mixing ratios (60% text / 25% image / 15% audio), and SDE-based batch-size reparameterization.
paperTitle: The Design Space of Tri-Modal Masked Diffusion Models
keywords: [multimodal diffusion, masked diffusion, scaling laws, text-to-image, text-to-speech]
content:
## Tri-Modal Masked Diffusion: Design Space Analysis

First large-scale study of tri-modal discrete diffusion. Systematically evaluates:
- Scaling laws (model size vs performance)
- Modality mixing ratios for balanced learning
- Noise schedules (discrete vs continuous)
- Batch size effects via SDE reparameterization

## Key Findings

| Design Choice | Optimal Value | Effect |
|---------------|---------------|--------|
| Modality mix | 60% text, 25% img, 15% audio | Balanced multimodal capability |
| Noise schedule | Cosine (discrete) | Better convergence than linear |
| Batch size coupling | Decoupled via SDE | Enables flexible scaling |
| Model size | 3B-7B | Diminishing returns >7B |

## Implementation Sketch

```python
def train_trimodal_diffusion(model, text_data, image_data, audio_data,
                             text_weight=0.6, image_weight=0.25, audio_weight=0.15):
    """Train on mixed modalities with optimal ratios"""
    text_sampler = iter(text_data)
    image_sampler = iter(image_data)
    audio_sampler = iter(audio_data)

    for step in range(num_steps):
        # Sample from modalities according to weights
        batch = []
        batch.extend(sample(text_sampler, int(batch_size * text_weight)))
        batch.extend(sample(image_sampler, int(batch_size * image_weight)))
        batch.extend(sample(audio_sampler, int(batch_size * audio_weight)))

        # Train masked diffusion
        loss = model.compute_masked_diffusion_loss(batch)
        loss.backward()
```
